codeReview.summary: The rewrite correctly moves the wrapper onto `just-bash`'s AST, keeps the tool-layer integration intact, and adds substantial new coverage. However, the AST walk is still incomplete: it never inspects `SimpleCommand.assignments`, and its recursive word-part scan only descends through `DoubleQuoted`, so executable substitutions can still slip through assignment values, parameter-expansion default words, and brace-expansion items under an allow-listed head.

issues.description: `checkSimpleCommand()` validates `cmd.name`, `cmd.args`, and `cmd.redirections`, but it never walks `cmd.assignments`. In just-bash, inputs like `FOO=$(whoami) ls` parse as a `SimpleCommand` named `ls` with an `AssignmentNode.value` containing `CommandSubstitution`, so the wrapper will forward the invocation whenever `ls` is allow-listed. The pre-AST scanner rejected raw `$(` anywhere in the command text, so this is a real allow-list bypass rather than a harmless message change.

`walkWordParts()` only recurses into `DoubleQuoted` and explicitly treats `ParameterExpansion` and `BraceExpansion` as safe leaves. just-bash nests executable `WordNode`s under parameter-expansion operations (for example `${X:-$(whoami)}` / `${X:=...}`) and under `BraceExpansion.items[].word`, so command, arithmetic, or process substitutions inside those nodes still execute in args/redirection targets beneath an allow-listed head. That violates the feature brief's "any nesting depth" requirement and reopens cases the old raw scanner would have rejected.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The new architecture note now codifies the same incomplete AST traversal rule as the implementation: it says the walker only needs to scan `args` and `redirections[].target`, and it explicitly states that `ParameterExpansionPart` in args is safe. Because just-bash nests executable words under `AssignmentNode.value`, `ParameterExpansion.operation.word`, and `BraceExpansion.items[].word`, future workers following the shared note will repeat the current allow-list blind spots.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/trieloff/Developer/slicc/.yolo/droid-2/.factory/library/architecture.md:58-65 documents the walker as only checking args/redirections and says `ParameterExpansionPart` in args is allowed; packages/webapp/src/tools/bash-tool-allowlist.ts:215-258 and 340-368 mirror that incomplete traversal.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance still disagrees on whether pure utility-wrapper features must perform live-browser validation. The mission and the worker skill both treat CDP verification as optional for this kind of feature, while the shared user-testing doc still says workers and validators MUST run the live-browser surface. This worker skipped CDP verification and documented the skip, so future workers and reviewers can still interpret the policy differently.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/trieloff/.factory/missions/094c0314-6896-4f76-bf58-89b7dfee66f4/mission.md:115-125 says the browser smoke test is optional and that no browser UI testing is required here; /Users/trieloff/Developer/slicc/.yolo/droid-2/.factory/skills/webapp-worker/SKILL.md:85-96 says CDP checks are OPTIONAL for pure utility wrappers; /Users/trieloff/Developer/slicc/.yolo/droid-2/.factory/library/user-testing.md:19-41 says workers and validators MUST perform live-browser validation; /Users/trieloff/.factory/missions/094c0314-6896-4f76-bf58-89b7dfee66f4/handoffs/2026-04-16T23-44-40-237Z__rewrite-bash-allowlist-on-just-bash-ast__1213770f-2849-436f-b0d7-d22539e6acd9.json:10-12 documents the intentional skip.

summary: Fail. Commit `f5523a2` successfully replaces the hand-rolled scanner with a just-bash AST walker and adds a large set of regression tests, but it still misses executable substitutions in assignment values and in nested word-bearing nodes such as parameter-expansion defaults and brace-expansion items. Those gaps leave real allow-list bypasses under an allow-listed head, so the feature does not yet preserve the previous rejection semantics or the promised any-depth substitution coverage.
